jokes.text: And if you haven't, you dirty miserable... Come on in here.
jokes.setup: Host welcoming viewers who haven't watched before
jokes.punchline: Starts insulting them mid-sentence then catches himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Danny's abrasive personality even when hosting. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: What do you mean busting my chops here, making believe you're a regular person.
jokes.setup: Elaine politely introduces herself as new driver
jokes.punchline: Louie assumes any politeness is mockery
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Louie's paranoid, hostile worldview. The phrase 'making believe you're a regular person' is brilliantly specific.

jokes.text: He'll give you the scales off his back.
jokes.setup: Trying to reassure Elaine that Louie is nice
jokes.punchline: Malapropism of 'shirt off his back' referencing Louie's reptilian nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant malapropism that works on multiple levels - sounds plausible while perfectly describing Louie's appearance.

jokes.text: I only work 60 hours a week.
jokes.punchline: 60 hours being called 'part-time'
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about economic reality. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Me? I'm a cabdriver. I'm the only cabdriver in this place.
jokes.punchline: The only honest one about being a cabdriver in a taxi company
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Alex's grounded realism versus everyone else's delusions.

jokes.text: Why is everyone here just a little angry?
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement calling the rage 'a little angry'
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor. Elaine's innocent perspective makes the dysfunction visible.

jokes.text: I am the living embodiment of the '60s.
jokes.punchline: Grandiose self-description that's both earnest and ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim dialogue - simultaneously profound and absurd, delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Everything that came along, I went with, even if I didn't know what it was, I went with it.
jokes.punchline: Admitting he blindly followed everything without understanding
jokes.explanation: Great character insight showing Jim's well-meaning but mindless approach to life.

jokes.text: Thank you.
jokes.setup: Tony angrily confronting Jim about Vietnam protests
jokes.punchline: Jim's sincere, simple gratitude defusing the tension
jokes.explanation: Masterful comedic beat. Two words completely defuse a heated moment through Jim's genuine innocence. Perfect economy and character work.

jokes.text: You're welcome.
jokes.punchline: Tony's automatic polite response despite his anger
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to Jim's 'thank you.' Shows Tony's fundamental decency overriding his anger.

jokes.text: Cabdriver.
jokes.setup: What kind of job could they get the old man with no skills?
jokes.punchline: The obvious answer they're all avoiding
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure. The inevitable conclusion everyone was avoiding.

jokes.text: Hasn't everyone?
jokes.setup: Asked about loss of consciousness, hallucinations, etc.
jokes.punchline: Treating serious medical conditions as universal experiences
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor. The casual acceptance of what should be alarming symptoms.

jokes.text: That's a tough choice.
jokes.setup: Mental illness or narcotic addiction question
jokes.punchline: Treating it as a difficult decision rather than denial
jokes.explanation: Solid follow-up to the previous joke. Shows character's casual relationship with dysfunction.

jokes.text: What... does... a... yellow... light... mean? repeated increasingly slowly
jokes.punchline: Increasingly slow repetition suggesting the test-taker is incredibly slow
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation with timing-based humor

jokes.text: It doesn't get any better.
jokes.setup: Watching the old man's driving test disaster
jokes.punchline: Understated assessment of hopeless situation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction. Alex's dry delivery caps the absurdist sequence.

jokes.text: You could've said you were looking for another apartment.
jokes.setup: Alex admits he was looking for her specifically
jokes.punchline: Angela immediately offering him an excuse to lie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Angela's self-protective defensiveness and low self-esteem.

jokes.text: Well, my admirers will just have to wander the halls looking for me.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reference to non-existent admirers
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent self-deprecating humor. Establishes Angela's bitter outlook.

jokes.text: Maybe Angela ate her.
jokes.setup: Alex wondering what happened to the nice girl on the phone
jokes.punchline: Elaine suggesting cannibalism as explanation
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected dark humor. Elaine's practical approach to Alex's romantic problems.

jokes.text: Now feel free to jump in and stop me anytime.
jokes.punchline: Inviting Alex to contradict her self-criticism
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Angela's self-awareness makes the moment both funny and painful.

jokes.text: I wouldn't mind looking like you if I was a man.
jokes.setup: Alex saying he's no Robert Redford
jokes.punchline: Angela's qualified compliment with gender caveat
jokes.explanation: Clever backhanded compliment that's somehow both insulting and flattering.

jokes.text: dropped ten or 70 pounds...
jokes.punchline: Massive range suggesting extreme weight loss needed
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation humor. The huge range reveals her self-perception.

jokes.text: You could just nod.
jokes.setup: Asking if she'd be romantically attractive
jokes.punchline: Offering Alex an easy way out of answering
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Angela's self-protection mechanism creates awkward humor.

jokes.text: I haven't been romantically involved in eight years and I'm not about to break my streak right now.
jokes.punchline: Treating celibacy as an achievement to maintain
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with nice 'streak' framing that makes celibacy sound like a sports record.

jokes.text: Make you feel like you did your good deed for the day?
jokes.setup: Alex wants her to open up
jokes.punchline: Reduces his motives to charity work
jokes.explanation: Angela's defensive cynicism about others' motivations

jokes.text: I can't believe you came all the way back here just because you forgot to give me the 'let's be friends' speech.
jokes.punchline: Angela recognizing and naming the clichéd gesture
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor. Angela's cynicism cuts through romantic comedy tropes.

jokes.text: Hey, Angela, I think we had a normal moment there.
jokes.punchline: Alex celebrating basic human interaction as achievement
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment. Alex's excitement over minimal progress with Angela is both funny and touching.

jokes.text: A half an hour.
jokes.setup: Alex asking how long since she cried
jokes.punchline: Revealing she cries constantly
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy beat. The brevity makes it funnier than a longer timeframe would.

jokes.text: Conskufflement.
jokes.punchline: Latka's made-up word for 'consummation'
jokes.explanation: Classic Latka malapropism. The made-up word perfectly captures his character while being inherently funny.

jokes.text: Either you have sex with me or you freeze to death. Sex. Death.
jokes.setup: Simka explaining the choice to Alex
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex situation to binary choice with dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist comedy with perfect dramatic delivery. The pause makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Talk is cheap. I want your body.
jokes.punchline: Simka's direct rejection of conversation
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection. Simka's directness subverts expected romantic comedy dialogue.

jokes.text: See? Smooth, milky white. Yours.
jokes.punchline: Offering her body parts like merchandise
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Simka's transactional approach to seduction is both funny and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Do you mock us because you do not eat animals with cloven hooves unless they chew their cud? No!
jokes.punchline: Simka citing kosher laws as comparison
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist argument. Simka's defense through religious comparison is unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: Now, peel me like a grape, so I can get out of here!
jokes.punchline: Ending philosophical argument with fruit metaphor demand
jokes.explanation: Fantastic absurdist line. The fruit metaphor combined with urgency creates perfect comedy.

jokes.text: Why don't they just get married again?
jokes.punchline: Jim's innocent suggestion of obvious solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment. His simple wisdom cuts through complex religious drama.

jokes.text: You son of a gun, you!
jokes.punchline: Tony's enthusiastic approval before hearing the problem
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the revelation. Tony's enthusiasm makes the coming awkwardness funnier.

jokes.text: Well, she wasn't the one I was after, Tony.
jokes.punchline: Revealing Tony was his target, not Elaine
jokes.explanation: Excellent surprise revelation. The misdirection is perfectly set up and pays off beautifully.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no, no...
jokes.punchline: Tony's gradual horrified realization
jokes.explanation: Good cringe reaction. Tony's dawning horror creates uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: I'm the one he spends tortured nights dreaming about.
jokes.setup: Tony explaining why he can't be alone with Kirk
jokes.punchline: Tony's dramatic assumption about Kirk's feelings
jokes.explanation: Good escalation. Tony's melodramatic interpretation of the situation fits his character.

jokes.text: He said that?
jokes.punchline: Alex's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man work. Alex's confusion grounds Tony's fantasy.

jokes.text: No, I'm just assuming.
jokes.setup: Alex asking if Kirk actually said that
jokes.punchline: Tony admitting he made it up
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Tony's drama. His casual admission undercuts his previous intensity.

jokes.text: It's okay with me.
jokes.punchline: Tony's permission giving for something he can't control
jokes.explanation: Good character beat. Tony treating it like he has authority over Alex's choices.

jokes.text: Alex, Elaine's heart is getting broken and you're having a great time here, dancing.
jokes.setup: Alex enjoying himself at gay bar
jokes.punchline: Tony pointing out the ironic situation
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation. Tony highlighting the absurd priorities in the situation.
